dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8035 IH: the Reforming Intelligence and Securing America Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Crawford introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend the authorities of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 through October 20, 2027, and for other purposes.

section.header: Extension of authorities of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978

subsection.header: Extension of repeal date of title VII
subsection.text: Section 403(b) of the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 (Public Law 110–261) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1) (50 U.S.C. 1881 note), by striking effective two years after and all that follows through the period at the end and inserting effective October 20, 2027, title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (50 U.S.C. 1881 et seq.) is repealed.; and

in paragraph (2) (18 U.S.C. 2511 note), in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by striking Effective two years after the date of enactment of the Reforming Intelligence and Securing America Act— and inserting Effective October 20, 2027—.

subsection.text: Section 404(b) of the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 (Public Law 110–261) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in the heading, by striking two years after the date of enactment of the reforming intelligence and securing america act and inserting repeal date; and
by striking , as amended by section 101(a) and by the FISA Amendments Reauthorization Act of 2017 and the Reforming Intelligence and Securing America Act, and inserting (50 U.S.C. 1881 et seq.); and

paragraph.text: in paragraph (2), by striking , as amended by section 101(a) and by the FISA Amendments Reauthorization Act of 2017 and the Reforming Intelligence and Securing America Act, and inserting (50 U.S.C. 1881 et seq.).

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall take effect on the earlier of the date of the enactment of this Act or April 19, 2026.
